commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For the wicked boasts of his heart's desire, and blesses the covetous, whom the LORD abhors. Augustine of Hippo 1. "They are taken in their thoughts, which they think:" that is, their evil thoughts become chains to them. But how become they chains. "For the sinner is praised," saith he, "in the desires of his soul" (ver. The tongues of flatterers bind souls in sin. For there is pleasure in doing those things, in which not only is no reprover feared, but even an approver heard. "And he that does unrighteous deeds is blessed. " Hence "are they taken in their thoughts, which they think.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
